inline_comments.message: As discussed in mail - the image version to be tested should be unambiguously specified (you can't be sure what you get with the default "latest", especially if there are multiple branches producing this image)
tabs to spaces (there are quite a few of these elsewhere as well)
image version (for all images in this file)?

This seems wrong. I can see that most of the other projects are defaulting to latest tag also.
The fact that we need to maintain legacy branches in the jjb's causes this issue.

If we are specifying versions then it would require a patch to the csits every time an image is released.

Which projects? Each and every one that pulls "latest" images blindly from Nexus is doing it wrong and exposes itself to these same risks.

Hardcoding isn't necessary if, for example, the CSITs refer to the project's version.properties when determining the version to pull.

Most of dcae, aai, sdnc to name just a few.
For access to the projects version.props it would require migration of the suite to the project repo right? Or pull the repo as part of setup?

In the context of this discussion I'm assuming we're talking about CSIT remaining in integration/csit (if you move the CSITs under project as described in https://wiki.onap.org/display/DW/Project-specific+CSIT+structure, this whole issue disappears), and in such setup the version would be acquired by pulling proper branch of the repo. 

As for the other projects:
- aai does not have any CSITs running in Jenkins so they have been neglected for a long time
- dcaegen2 is a huge mass of tests I've wanted to look into closer as there are multiple issues there with image versioning, but I can't currently hope to find the time to dig into those  
- sdnc performs a bit of sleight of hand there - technically it always ends up testing images that are labeled just "latest", but it actually pulls specific STAGING versions from Nexus and relabels them in setup.sh (to simplify docker-compose I assume)

Yes they will remain here for now. DR could be moved but MR and BC cannot yet due to +1 repos for each.

I completely understand your frustration here and I am looking for a solution.
The fact that pm-mapper is broken due to the "latest" tagging in docker-stage is an issue.
This will break the BC suite also.
Could there be a way to disable the cron jobs for the older branches and maybe add a magic word to trigger if needed?
